title: Make a Real API Call
instructions: Call the Claude API using curl or the SDK. Paste the `id` field from the response (starts with `msg_`).
hint: Run: curl https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages with your API key

title: Call the Anthropic API
instructions: Send a message to Claude via the API and paste the response message ID.
hint: The response JSON contains an `id` field starting with `msg_`

title: Verify Claude Code Installation
instructions: Run `claude --version` in your terminal and paste the full output.
hint: If not installed, run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

title: Run Your First Claude Code Session
instructions: Run `claude -p "What directory am I in?"` and paste the output.
hint: The -p flag runs Claude Code in print/headless mode

title: List Your MCP Servers
instructions: Run `claude mcp list` in your terminal and paste the output.
hint: This shows all configured MCP servers. It's OK if the list is empty.

title: Configure an MCP Server
instructions: Add an MCP server to your project and paste the contents of your `.mcp.json` or MCP config.
hint: Try: claude mcp add filesystem -- npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem

title: Check Supabase CLI
instructions: Run `supabase --version` and paste the output to verify the Supabase CLI is installed.

title: Verify Git Setup
instructions: Run `git config --global user.name && git config --global user.email` and paste the output.
hint: This confirms your git identity is configured for commits

title: Store an API Key Safely
instructions: Run `echo $ANTHROPIC_API_KEY | head -c 15` and paste the output (first 15 chars only — never share the full key).

title: Create a Next.js Project
instructions: Run `npx create-next-app@latest --typescript test-project` and paste the terminal output showing the project was created.
hint: This scaffolds a new Next.js project with TypeScript
